summary: postinstall script invokes child_process and HTTP requests — needs human review
details: The package ships a postinstall.js script that imports child_process and performs HTTP GET requests referencing a hostname. Pattern-matching on these primitives co-occurring in a lifecycle script is a known shape of install-time droppers, but the available evidence does not include the destination URL, the data being read, or what is done with any fetched content. Without confirming whether the script fetches and executes attacker-controlled bytes, scrapes installer secrets, or simply performs a benign vendor-binary download, intent cannot be determined from the matched lines alone. Routing to human review for inspection of postinstall.js to determine the fetch destination, payload disposition (data vs. exec), and whether installer-side resources are read or modified.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
